title: A hydrokinetic power generator
abstract: A hydrokinetic generator including: a submersible housing defining a conduit therethrough for the flow of a fluid; a turbine mounted to the housing comprising at least one impeller located in the conduit for rotation by said flow; and at least one electrical generator coupled to the at least one turbine for converting mechanical energy from the turbine to electrical energy, the electrical generator including a plurality of elongate members bearing one or more magnetic regions, the elongate members being disposed about the at least one impeller and fast therewith; and a number of windings located within material of the housing and arranged for electromagnetic interaction with said magnetic regions whereby in use rotation of the impeller moves the magnetic regions past the windings to thereby induce an electrical current in the windings. The impeller may comprise a plurality of spiral, helical blades disposed about a common axle from a leading end thereof to a trailing end wherein a radius of the blades increases exponentially from the leading end to the trailing end.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1 . An impeller for a hydrokinetic turbine comprising a plurality of spiral, helical blades disposed about a common axle from a leading end thereof to a trailing end wherein the blades' radii increases from the leading end to the trailing end. 2. An impeller according to claim 1 , wherein the blades' radii increases exponentially from the leading end to the trailing end. 3. An impeller according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein ribs are disposed over the blades to assist in capturing energy from fluid flowing across the impeller in use. 4. An impeller according to claim 3, wherein the ribs comprise one or more radial ribs and one or more spiral ribs. 5. An impeller according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the surface of the ribs is roughened to assist in capturing energy from fluid flowing across the impeller in use. 6 A hydrokinetic generator including: a submersible housing defining a conduit therethrough for the flow of a fluid; a turbine mounted to the housing comprising at least one impeller according to any one of claims 1 to 5, located in the conduit for rotation by said flow; and at least one electrical generator coupled to the at least one turbine for converting mechanical energy from the turbine to electrical energy. 7. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 6, wherein the turbine comprises first and second impellers each having a leading end and a trailing end, the impellers being mounted coaxially with trailing ends adjacent to each other for accommodating bidirectional flows through the conduit. 8. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 6 or claim 7 including a plurality of magnetic regions disposed about the at least one impeller and fast therewith; and a number of windings arranged for electromagnetic interaction with said magnetic regions whereby in use rotation of the impeller moves the magnetic regions past the windings to thereby induce an electrical current in the windings. 9. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 8 including elongate members bearing one or more magnetic regions. 10. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 8, wherein an impeller ring is disposed about outermost tips of the impeller and fastened thereto wherein the impeller ring and the impeller are coaxial. 1 1 . A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 10, wherein the magnetic regions are disposed around the impeller ring. 12. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 9, wherein the elongate members bearing said magnetic regions extend from the impeller ring in parallel with the axis of the impeller. 13. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 10 or claim 12, including leading and trailing rings, wherein leading tips of the elongate members are fastened to the leading ring and trailing tips of the elongate members are fastened to the trailing ring. 14. A hydrokinetic generator according to any one of claims 10, 12 or 13, wherein a wall defining the conduit is formed with an annular recess for receiving a periphery of the impeller ring. 15. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 14, wherein leading and trailing cylindrical openings are formed in the wall which are continuous with the annular recess and extend laterally therefrom for accommodating the elongate members and the leading ring and trailing ring. 16. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 14 or claim 15, wherein windings are located within the wall. 17. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 16, wherein the windings are located on opposed sides of the leading and trailing cylindrical openings. 18. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 16 or claim 17, wherein the windings are sealed within material of the wall in order that they are isolated from contact with fluid flowing through the conduit in use. 19. A hydrokinetic generator according to any one of claims 8 to 18 including cables connected to the windings and impregnated within the housing which extend therethrough to a connection terminal. 20. A hydrokinetic generator according to any one of claims 8 to 19 wherein each of the windings has a ferromagnetic core. 21 . A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 7, wherein each impeller is mounted to an impeller frame. 22. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 21 , including a drive wheel pivotally connected between the impeller frames and rotationally fixed to the axle of each impeller. 23. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 21 or claim 22, wherein the electrical generator is mounted above the impeller frames. 24. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 23, wherein the electrical generator is mounted to a generator frame that is fastened to each of the impeller frames. 25. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 24, wherein the electrical generator includes a rotor that is coupled to a sheave for rotation thereby. 26. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 25, wherein the drive wheel is coupled to the sheave by one or more belts. 27. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 26 wherein the rotor of the generator is coupled to the sheave via a clutch that is arranged to disengage in response to the sheave being rotated above a predetermined rate. 28. A hydrokinetic generator according to any one of claims 6 to 7 and claims 19 to 25 wherein the housing forms part of a pontoon that is formed with a hull having the conduit therethrough in which the turbine is located. 29. A hydrokinetic generator according to claim 28, wherein one or more cowls are provided between the turbine and the electrical generator to reduce fluid contact with the generator. 30. A hydrokinetic generator including: a submersible housing defining a conduit therethrough for the flow of a fluid; a turbine mounted to the housing comprising at least one impeller located in the conduit for rotation by said flow; and at least one electrical generator coupled to the at least one turbine for converting mechanical energy from the turbine to electrical energy, the electrical generator including a ring about the impeller and fast therewith, the ring having one or more magnetic regions; and an annular recess formed in the housing and receving a periphery of the ring; a number of windings located within material of the housing adjacent the annular recess for electromagnetic interaction with said magnetic regions whereby in use rotation of the impeller moves the magnetic regions past the windings to thereby induce an electrical current in the windings.
